DECISION: adopt Goose (Apache-2.0, Block / Agentic AI Foundation @ Linux Foundation) as the spawnlet's agent. Speaks ACP, runs headless over a cwd with built-in file/shell tools, OpenAI provider accepts a custom base_url -> points at our sidecar. Implementation unknowns to confirm: exact Goose ACP launch invocation + how to inject the app persona/instructions + set cwd=/data. Captured in the spawnlet slice design.

Done in sp-pc4 part 3a (merged 0978c24): node_id persisted via SetActive + compensating cleanup on the post-Provision orphan window. (Was filed depending-on sp-pc4, but it's a completed sub-item; sp-pc4 stays open for part 3b.)

DONE merged d568c21. Wire-up: spawnlet.NewManagerWithBackend(pod,fw,cfg) injects backend+floor (NewManager delegates = Docker+DOCKER-USER); cmd/spawnlet buildManager(cfg) selects by CONTAINER_RUNTIME — runsc -> cri.NewCRIPodBackend(cri.Dial(CRI_ENDPOINT),CRI_RUNTIME_HANDLER)+firewall.NewCNIFloorApplier(); else Docker+NewManager. New env CRI_ENDPOINT/CRI_RUNTIME_HANDLER (deployment.md). Composed runsc path verified coherent end-to-end (final review): CRIPodBackend+CNIFloorApplier satisfy interfaces; StartPod sets PodIP/NetnsPath/SandboxID that floor+AttachACP+Stop consume; AttachACP backend-agnostic; floor applied identically via m.fw. Hermetic (lazy cri.Dial/NewDocker). The REAL runsc round-trip is host-only -> MANUAL_VERIFICATION.md sec N procedure (closes sp-vaw once run on a privileged containerd/runsc/CNI host). All 5 epic slices code-complete + merged.

DONE merged 80d1767. Per-backend ACP transport: Docker/runc lane = Docker stdio attach (rt.Attach, root-free, Mac+Linux+rootless Podman); CRI/runsc lane = UDS+setns (Linux+cloud). PodBackend.Attach + Manager.Attach routes the relay (Server.attach injection removed). Agent entrypoint toggles ACP_ADAPTER (Docker=goose PID1 attachable; CRI sets ACP_ADAPTER=1 -> adapter); stubagent direct. Empty pod IP tolerated (rootless Podman slirp4netns), Manager fail-closes only when egressEnforced()&&PodIP=='' (closes sp-mcb; CRI still requires IP). Image Go builders bumped to golang:1.26. Docs: CAP_SYS_ADMIN now CRI/cloud-only + rootless self-hosted matrix. RESULT: self-hosted Mac/Linux/rootless-Docker/rootless-Podman = NO root; cloud CRI = root for floor only. Final review: composes correctly, fail-closed invariant holds, no regression. Real Docker-attach-on-Mac + Podman ContainerAttach are host-verify.

SUBSUMED + DONE in sp-11p (merged 80d1767): DockerPodBackend.StartPod now best-effort on ContainerIP/PID; Manager.Create fail-closes only when egressEnforced()&&PodIP==''. Required for rootless Podman (no bridge IP). Tests: TestCreateEmptyIP{FailsClosedWhenEnforced,SucceedsWhenNotEnforced} + TestDockerPodBackendStartPodToleratesMissingIPAndPID.

Slice 1 backend complete: proto name+RenameSpawn, migration 0004, store.Rename, name autogen, Rename/Suspend/Resume handlers. 8 tasks, two-stage reviewed, final review READY TO MERGE. Merged to master (5fba342).

Slice 2 done: acpadapter recording proxy + spawn/history replay-on-attach; web client onHistory + historyToItems mapper. 3 tasks, two-stage reviewed, final review READY-TO-MERGE (cross-layer contract verified). Merged to master.
Slice 2 done (re-close after .beads merge-import reverted it): acpadapter recording proxy + spawn/history replay + client onHistory/historyToItems. Merged to master.
Slice 3 done: multi-spawn sidebar + App state (buffer switch-history, 3s poll, onHistory) + api + e2e (9/9 real containers). Merged to master (abff754).

All 3 demo-scope build slices done + merged (sp-zer backend, sp-02f adapter replay, sp-r6b web multi-spawn UI). Tests green (go + web unit/tsc + 9/9 e2e). Post-demo work tracked separately (sp-3nb durable history, sp-suc pagination, sp-1ni suspend reconcile).

Node-relay transcript recording + replay. Recorder extracted to internal/transcript (shared w/ adapter); node records each spawn's transcript at the relay + replays spawn/history on every client (re)connect, Docker lane only (lane-aware). History now survives a browser reload in just dev — proven by e2e (10/10, reload test wipes the client buffer so only node replay can restore). 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE.

WS status indicator shipped: useConnStatus hook (state machine + 5s slow timer) + ConnStatus component (gray-pulse connecting… / amber-pulse slow / green connected / red error, hidden when no live socket); wired into App/AppShell replacing the free-text status. Vitest (hook+component, incl. fake-timer slow + error-preserved-on-close) + tsc clean; e2e 10/10 (selectors -> 'connected'). 2 tasks two-stage reviewed. Merged to master.

Visible spawn starting lifecycle. CP CreateSpawn async (validate sync, store.Create starting, return id, background provisionSpawn -> SetActive/SetError on node signal, logs provision failures). Web: yellow starting dot, grey-pulse 'waiting' header, WS gated on active via poll (1s while starting else 3s) + pure nextConnAction policy, failed/unreachable spawns red+removable, chat input gated on connected. 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 10/10 (retries:2 absorbs ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED env flake). Merged to master.

Node readiness probe: startSpawn now sends an ACP initialize and awaits the response (probeReady, retry-attach to 30s for CRI adapter startup) before reporting ACTIVE; timeout->mgr.Stop+ERROR. So the UI's green dot means 'agent answered' not just 'container launched', and yellow/starting covers the ~5s goose boot. awaitInitialize unit-tested (6 cases); Manager.Stop made teardown cancellation-immune. 2 tasks two-stage reviewed; final whole-branch review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 11/11 clean (probe runs in the real node lane vs stub). Follow-ups: sp-r14 (probeReady retry-loop test). Merged to master.

WS auto-reconnect via partysocket. New reconnectingSocket.ts wraps PartySocket as a WebSocketLike that retries forever with an escalating per-attempt connect-timeout (500/2.5s/5s/15s/30s, success resets; via mutating partysocket _options.connectionTimeout on close, pinned 1.1.19 + structural guard). openSession re-runs the ACP handshake on every onOpen (node replays history); teardown close()s the socket; ledger poll owns connect/teardown while the wrapper owns WS-level reconnect. useConnStatus gained reconnecting (yellow) + disconnected (red after 12s grace). 3 tasks two-stage reviewed; final review READY-TO-MERGE; e2e 13/13 (also fixed pre-existing prompt-send drift from sp-95v -> Enter-to-send). Merged to master.

Long-lived per-spawn pump + multi-client session SHIPPED (Plans 1+2). Pump (internal/node) owns one goose ACP session, append-only frame log fanned out to N clients with resumable per-client cursors, broker turn/queue, transient permission broadcast (first-wins/timeout-deny). Integration: client_id+cursor proto; multi-client CP router; node attach rewired onto the pump (pump.start=readiness gate, replacing sp-39u probe; agent-death reclaims); web thin frame client (prompt/subscribe, per-tab clientId+cursor, no ACP). Fixes the reconnect-churn race (leaked relays/dropped inbox/stuck 'working...') structurally. 4+6 tasks two-stage reviewed; final reviews READY-TO-MERGE; full go suite + 77 web vitest + e2e 13/13 green. Subsumed sp-r7t (node owns one session) + sp-39u probe. Merged to master.

Pump-wiring interface reqs done in Plan 2: stop() calls AttachedStream.Close via closeFn; exitFn surfaces agent-death (ERROR + reclaim pump/active/container). Permission title still generic + the multi-client-detach-mid-in-flight-turn test remain minor follow-ups (see new cleanup bead). Done with sp-bjd.

Shipped: in-pod acpadapter is now a transparent byte-bridge with a bounded 1MiB gap buffer (oldest-whole-line eviction); node pump owns brokering/history/multi-client fan-out on the CRI lane, matching the Docker lane. internal/transcript deleted. Merged to master.

Superseded by the long-lived per-spawn pump (sp-bjd, merged). The pump owns the single goose ACP session for the spawn's whole life and issues 'initialize'+'session/new' exactly once; thin clients only send prompt frames and never re-handshake. The redundant-initialize/session-new-on-reconnect problem this bead targeted no longer exists — there is no per-connection relay re-doing the ACP handshake to filter. (CRI lane brought to the same model in sp-j5b.)

WS auto-reconnect shipped & merged: ReconnectingSocket (partysocket@1.1.19 pinned) with escalating connect-timeout 500ms/2.5s/5s/15s/30s, handshake re-runs per (re)connect, useConnStatus reconnecting(yellow)/disconnected(red after grace), guard+behavior tests. (Re-closing: today's branch checkout/merge reverted the original close commit 4f1de17 via the .beads import hook.)

Pump cleanup shipped: Conn trimmed to receive-only ndjson reader; orphaned web/src/acp/types.ts deleted; readyProbeTimeout->readyTimeout (+doc); vestigial Config.InPodAdapter removed; goose's real toolCall.title now surfaced on perm_request frames (broadcast+re-send, tested); cpStream seam + startSpawn create-failure lifecycle test added. Remaining attacher success-path/exitFn/attach-fail tests moved to sp-sj8. Merged to master.

Correctness-focused linters added (no formatting/style/deprecation churn). Backend: .golangci.yml (golangci-lint v2 standard set + bodyclose + nilerr; staticcheck drops ST/QF/SA1019; errcheck excludes idiomatic cleanup-closers); 6 intentional closers made explicit; 0 issues. Frontend: web/eslint.config.js (typescript-eslint recommended + react-hooks rules-of-hooks/exhaustive-deps; no-explicit-any off; no prettier); fixed 1 real no-constant-binary-expression in utils.test.ts, removed 2 stale eslint-disables, 1 documented disable; 0 problems. 'just lint' (lint-go + lint-web) green. golangci-lint must be go1.26-built (documented in Justfile). Deprecations tracked in sp-j7l.

All deliverables verified on disk: Makefile (bin/% + .make/ gen+image stamps), Justfile (spawnlet/cp/node/web/dev-mprocs/spawnctl/test*/setup/reap), mprocs.yaml committed, test* moved to just, /.make/ gitignored, README runs slice via just. Extended since with lint + egress recipes.

Orphan-container handling complete: labels + reap-on-startup (prior, 57c7aba) + idle reaper (this session). Items 2/3/4 of the remaining list landed TDD: generation fencing on Stop (node ignores stale-gen), two-stage idle reaper (15m detached/60m attached, lossy STOPPED), graceful SIGTERM teardown (Manager.StopAll wired via signal.NotifyContext; fixes sp-8mn e2e leak). Item 1 was already done via sp-pc4 reconcileInventory. Suite green, golangci-lint clean. Handed to sp-gd9: lossless suspend-on-idle (E3-gated) + the Suspend-message fence (helper ready).
Implemented + tested: DiscoverOrCreateSession (dir-scoped, resume-safe); adapter maps session/new onto it

Agent image built (opencode 1.15.13 + tmux + tini), entrypoint runs opencode serve + adapter with a generated sidecar-backed opencode provider config. VALIDATED end-to-end in a podman pod: real ACP prompt streamed 'PONG' via adapter->opencode->sidecar->OpenRouter.

Both lanes unified on TCP ACP. DockerPodBackend sets ACP_LISTEN=tcp + dials podIP:ACPPort via AttachTCP (mirrors CRI); stdio hijack removed; shared runtime.ACPPort; injectable port for tests. Tests reworked to TCP (loopback agent + transport-agnostic relay). Full suite green. VALIDATED end-to-end with the real image on rootful docker: host dialed pod bridge IP 172.17.0.6:7000 -> adapter -> opencode -> sidecar -> OpenRouter -> 'PONG'. (Episode-end FSM nuance tracked under sp-5h3.5; rootless-without-bridge-IP noted unsupported.)

Node terminal launcher done: StartTerminal runs mosh-server whose child execs into the spawn container (docker/crictl exec) running 'tmux new-session -A opencode attach <url> -s/-c' on the shared opencode session. ExecPrefixFor picks docker vs crictl by lane. Real mosh-server launch+parse validated (integration test); in-container tmux+opencode-attach renders the web's session (shared visibility) validated on real docker.
spawnctl tmux -spawn <id> implemented (standalone direct-to-node path; CP routing is sp-wsu.2). POSTs /terminal, execs mosh-client. Builds + suite green.

pki library complete + tested (commit b941061 on worktree branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova): Root CA, name-constrained cloud/self-hosted intermediates (self-hosted cryptographically can't forge cloud — proven load-bearing), IssueNode w/ SAN identity, Verify (chain+constraints+expiry)->Identity, PEM persistence, TLSCertificate. Offline-mint CLI + checked-in test-Root-CA fixture deferred to consumers (T9 e2e builds its own test CA via the API; a thin cmd/spawnery-ca wrapper can be added if/when ops needs it).

AS skeleton done (worktree branch, commit 972fac2): internal/authsvc Service holds the self-hosted intermediate + issues self-hosted-only certs (verify against published root), Load from PEM, HTTP Handler (/healthz + /ca/root export), cmd/authsvc container entrypoint w/ graceful shutdown. Enrollment-token auth = sp-0qc, AS-signed sessions = sp-3ca build on top. TDD green; build/vet/gofmt clean.

Enrollment vertical done (commit 563dd42): pki NewNodeCSR/SignCSR (proof-of-possession; CA-chosen SAN) + CSR PEM; authsvc IssueEnrollmentToken (one-time, account-bound, ~10m, atomic consume), Enroll, POST /enroll, RunEnroll node client. Cert bound to token's account + node's own key, class always self-hosted. Owner OAuth gating = E4 (out of scope; token endpoint not exposed unauthenticated). TDD green; build/vet/gofmt clean.

CP enforcement done (commit fd2a12a): nodeauth core + runNode takes id from verified cert (ignores self-asserted Register when authenticated; insecure keeps fallback) + cmd/cp NODE_AUTH_MODE insecure(h2c, default)/enforced(dedicated mTLS listener, RequireAnyClientCert + middleware verify vs pinned root). Full proto-field REMOVAL deferred until enforced-only (would break node sender + insecure tests); fields are ignored-when-authenticated for now. TDD green (TestRegisterPrefersVerifiedIdentity); full repo suite 28 pkgs green.

Tenancy gate done (commit ec77df3): registry.PickFor enforces cloud=multi-tenant / self-hosted=owner-only; placementFor returns {Owner} for all tiers (review tier only gates unverified-author authorization). Fixes latent bug (reviewed app could land on another owner's self-hosted node). Node-side defense-in-depth refusal -> sp-1g3 (needs node cert identity). TDD TestPickForTenancy; full repo 28 green.

Node mTLS done (commit 8c7992b): internal/node/nodeid (Save/Load identity, MTLSClient = h2 client w/ node cert + pinned CP root); cmd/spawnlet nodeCPClient picks transport by NODE_AUTH_MODE (insecure=h2c/CP_ADDR; enforced=load-or-enroll identity, mTLS to CP_NODE_ADDR). Node-side tenancy refusal needs spawn-owner in StartSpawn (contract change) -> deferred; CP gate authoritative. TDD green; full repo 29.

AS session-token MECHANISM done (commit 6b34091): internal/sessiontoken Ed25519 Sign/Verify (offline; rejects tamper/wrong-key/expiry) + authsvc session key/IssueSessionToken/SessionPubKey + /session/pubkey. Live-flow consumption (CP issue on session-open, node verify + self-hosted/cloud split on attach) = follow-up sp-rjr (spans WS + node relay + web, overlaps E4). TDD green; build/vet/fmt clean.

Client verification PRIMITIVE done (commit f8c6339): clientverify.VerifyHost checks host node cert chains to pinned root + SAN matches expectation (self-hosted==my account / cloud multi-tenant). TDD covers the §7 attacks (foreign self-hosted, forged cloud, class mismatch). Consume-side wiring (spawnctl/node call it on a CP-vended node cert) depends on cert-vending = sp-gtm/sp-zdd; web delivery = sp-ore. Build/vet/fmt clean.

PKI-soundness e2e done (commit 3f56186): real mTLS handshake vs enforced middleware accepts valid self-hosted+cloud, rejects forged-cloud/expired/wrong-root/no-cert; forged session token rejected; clientverify host-binding. Full repo 31 pkgs green; golangci-lint 0 issues.

All 9 children closed. Node-auth + Auth Service implemented end-to-end TDD on branch worktree-node-auth-sp-ova (9 commits b941061..3f56186): pki name-constrained CA, authsvc (enrollment + AS sessions), CP cert-derived identity + insecure/enforced modes + mTLS listener, tenancy gate, node mTLS dial, sessiontoken, clientverify, PKI-soundness e2e. Full repo 31 pkgs green; golangci-lint 0 issues; insecure default keeps dev/tests unchanged. Follow-ups filed: sp-rjr (live session-flow wiring), and consume-side client verify depends on sp-zdd/sp-gtm cert-vending + sp-ore web delivery.

Implemented: registry per-connection token + lastBeat; Register rejects duplicate id when existing node is alive (heartbeat <15s), silently displaces a dead one; runNode teardown token-guarded (RemoveIfCurrent) + displaced streams stop (IsCurrent). Node-side fail-fast on terminal-port bind already added for the same-host case. Unit tests cover reject-alive/displace-dead/epoch-guard.

Single agent image delivered: opencode+goose+claude+nori+tmux embedded; dispatcher entrypoint keyed by runnable name; opencode-served/opencode-tui/goose-tui wired + e2e-verified. Remaining per-agent wiring tracked as follow-ups: claude converter sp-9xr.15, goose-acp bridge sp-9xr.16, nori ACP-TUI client sp-9xr.12. AGENT_BINARIES advertises wired agents per node config.

Superseded: the docker ENTRYPOINT-vs-Cmd argv issue is resolved by the argv-aware entrypoint (sp-9xr.5) + the dispatcher entrypoint keyed by runnable name (sp-9xr.13b), where the image owns launch and the node passes the runnable id.

Anthropic<->OpenAI converter in sidecar (/v1/messages: request + non-stream + streaming SSE incl tool_use), claude-tui dispatcher case wired (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL->sidecar), verified live via TestCPClaudeTuiEndToEnd (real Claude Code tool round-trip). Reviewed APPROVED.

goose-acp wired via new generic acpexec stdio-ACP<->TCP bridge (listens on ACP_LISTEN, runs 'goose acp' piped to the node's Pump connection, fresh child per connection, deadlock-safe via StdinPipe/StdoutPipe). goose pointed at OpenAI sidecar (same env as goose-tui). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd reaches ACTIVE (ACP handshake) + real model output ('banana') via Pump fanout. Reviewed: 1 blocking deadlock found+fixed (cmd.Wait blocking on net.Conn copy goroutines when child exits first) + regression test added.

acpmux: in-container single-session ACP multiplexer delivered. Spawns 'goose acp' (one shared upstream session S), presents canonical ACP on :7000 multiplexing N downstream clients (replaces acpexec for goose-acp); replay-log+cursor for late joiners, notification fanout, serialized prompts (exact per-client result correlation), permission broadcast first-wins. Ported from internal/node/pump.go; node pump unchanged + transparent. Review found+fixed 1 blocking ordering race (turn-end result racing session/update chunks) via single-writer ordered log (TestFanout 800/800), + 4 minors (prompt-error forwarding, Stop closes conns, id-0 sentinel, perm-ordering documented cosmetic). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpEndToEnd (transparent through acpmux) + TestAcpmuxTwoClientsShareSession (fanout+late-join replay, real goose) PASS.

spawnctl attach to acp-mode spawn launches nori joining the SHARED goose session. acpdial stdio<->TCP shim (nori's custom 'spawnery' agent -> acpmux:7000); nori config baked ([[agents]] name+slug + [agents.distribution.local].command=/usr/local/bin/acpdial). terminalInnerCmd acp branch -> nori argv (spawnctl unchanged). Found+fixed load-bearing bug: nori uses STRING JSON-RPC ids; acp.Message.ID *int silently dropped nori's initialize -> introduced RawID (verbatim string|int round-trip; int helpers for self-assigned ids) across acp/pump/acpmux/ocadapter/stubagent. Reviewed APPROVED (RawID contained to agent-facing ACP wire; web Frame protocol untouched). Verified live: TestCPGooseAcpNoriAttach PASS — nori rendered real 'banana' via nori->acpdial->acpmux->goose AND a 2nd client late-joined+replayed nori's turn (shared session). The remaining transport — CP relay of sealed secrets to a live node + sidecar tmpfs injection — is proto-dependent (see sp-2ckv.4 + new proto issue).

Backward-compat: legacy unbound path retained (follow-up to remove). 10 new tests + existing green.

Sealed-secret delivery DONE e2e + full -race green (master). Proto: signed_subkey on Register/Heartbeat, CPMessage.SecretDelivery+SealedSecret, cp.v1 GetSpawnNodeKey/DeliverSecrets. Owner→CP(ciphertext-only relay)→node OpenDelivered→tmpfs 0600 at target_path; CP caches node sub-key+cert chain, never unseals; per-spawn secrets tmpfs at /run/spawnery/secrets (never-persist §6). version/deliveryId AAD = follow-up hooks for sp-u53.5.4. NOTE: the JOURNAL-key consumer swap (journal repo password over this path) is sp-u53.5.4.

DONE + full -race green (master). OwnerSealedCustody serves a delivered repo password via the merged SecretDelivery path (secret_id journal/<mount>); internal/secrets/journalkey (SealToOwner/ResealForNode), internal/cp/journalkeys (ciphertext store + owner-device-registry SEAM — real registry + Put/Get wired by the migrate slice sp-u53.5.3). Node routes journal-key deliveries; spawnlet waits for the key before Restore. Origin path keeps node-local mint then seals same password to owner. Race suite caught+fixed a self-deadlock.

MigrateSpawn DONE + full -race green (master). cp.v1 MigrateSpawn(spawn_id,target) + Get/PutJournalKeyCiphertext. Claim-guarded suspend(sp-a7fs markers)+resume-with-placement under one per-spawn lock; resume-failure→RevertSuspended (data-safe). Placement override (TargetNodeID/RequireClass) honored by PickFor atop tenancy; OwnerDeviceRegistry→fail-closed MemDeviceRegistry. spawnctl move orchestrates owner-side journal-key reseal→deliver (CP ciphertext-only). Cross-node restore validated at unit/crypto level; real 2-node e2e + web 'Move to…' button = out of scope (web). Production target-subkey PKI verify = documented hook.

Proto work DONE across the delivery+migrate slices: sealed-secret delivery message (SecretDelivery/SealedSecret + signed_subkey) landed in sp-2ckv.4; MigrateSpawn + journal-key RPCs in sp-u53.5.3; suspend/generation/markers proto already existed. buf toolchain available in dev-spawnery distrobox. Phase ② core DONE + race + live garage_e2e (master). Continuous watcher (fsnotify+darwin seam, periodic+ENOSPC-rescan fallbacks) drives RequestSnapshot, wired in spawnlet. Per-gen Garage key fence (GarageAdmin+GenerationKeyManager): live e2e mint→write→revoke→denied. Manager.FullMaintenance (M5 node primitive) + telemetry seam. Deeper protocol-threading scoped to children: sp-ik70 (per-gen backend StartSpawn threading), sp-k17x (CP full-maintenance cmd), sp-yb2a (degraded modes M13), sp-maex (prune anchor M7 + telemetry).

ALL PASS. apt/useradd/chown green w/ DEFAULT caps under userns-remap (no custom cap list); 1.19GB commit=4.0s w/ ~93ms block; layer tar excludes mounts+tmpfs, OCI .wh. whiteouts; save de-shifts uids -> artifact mapping-agnostic, A(700000)->B(800000) round-trip clean; chown-into-range replaces 0777; default caps can't touch floor iptables, NET_ADMIN can (invariant: never grant agent NET_ADMIN). Gotcha: /etc/hosts|resolv.conf|hostname are engine bind mounts, never captured. Podman: default rootless green; --userns=auto broken on 5.8.2+crun. Findings: docs/superpowers/notes/2026-06-12-writable-rootfs-spike-plan.md

ALL ANSWERED. (1) CONFIRMED: runsc default --overlay2 (root:self) swallows writes — host upperdir holds only the sentry-private .gvisor.filestore blob; host diff would capture nothing useful. (2) overlay2=none -> full host visibility (files + kernel char-dev whiteouts); gofer cost NEGLIGIBLE for our workloads (apt install 3.6s vs 3.3s default; dd 256M ~equal). network=host (hostinet) needed in runsc.toml for host-net pods + CRI dns_config (systemd-resolved stub unreachable from netstack). (3) Diff via containerd DiffService from CRI container id (= snapshot key, k8s.io ns): proper OCI .wh. whiteouts, container-relative uids. (4) Cross-lane: docker-save bundle from remapped daemon imports via ImportIndex and runs under runsc — package/user/uids intact.

ANSWERED. KEP-127 pod userns from bare CRI client: PASS on runc (containerd 2.2.3 constructs userns 0->900000:65536, setgroups=allow, apt/useradd/chown/setuid green; container userns_options must match sandbox; hostNetwork rejected - CNI required). KEP-127 + runsc: BROKEN (gofer setns into pinned userns -> EINVAL, multithreaded Go cannot join userns). BUT runsc does not need kernel userns: sentry virtualizes privilege natively (uid_map 0->0:4G), full workload green with default caps, gofer writes container-relative uids host-side. Net: cloud lane = runsc + overlay2=none + default caps, no kernel userns. Findings in spike-plan note.

Design spec written: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-writable-rootfs-survival-design.md (spike-verified: Docker lane userns-remap+default-caps+commit; runsc lane overlay2=none, no kernel userns; one OCI delta artifact; pinned digests; staged S0->S2). Impl tasks created: sp-ei4.1.7-13 with deps.
Docker-lane userns-remap node mode: CapPolicy (DropAll|DefaultSet), daemon probe with degraded fallback, remap base uid exposed on Manager

Implemented node/spawnlet migration artifact production and restore: node proto carries RootfsArtifact pins plus Suspend.capture_rootfs_artifact; source migration suspend exports the captured delta and stores it as a generation-keyed journal rootfs_delta artifact; target StartSpawn restores only CP-pinned artifact IDs for the source generation, validates generation/base digest, imports the delta before launch, and normal same-node Suspend remains local-only. added string error field (5) to SuspendComplete proto; make gen regenerated; gates green
PodBackend Pause/Unpause added to docker + cri backends; ContainerRuntime seam + FakeRuntime record calls; delta_test.go asserts agent container is targeted
split suspend into SnapshotForSuspend gate (pause+snapshot, abort/restore on failure) and FinishSuspend (teardown); tests for abort and success paths
route SuspendComplete.error to FailedPrecondition, leave spawn ACTIVE on suspend failure
reorder suspendSpawn to gate->reap->finish; emit SuspendComplete{error} on gate fail, keep spawn ACTIVE

Fail-closed suspend implemented + merged (sp-ei4.1.15.1-5, all green on master). Pause/Unpause on PodBackend (docker + cri); manager SnapshotForSuspend gate (pause->snapshot; fail=unpause+restart watchers+keep spawn, success=stay paused) + FinishSuspend teardown; node suspendSpawn reordered gate->reap->finish, emits SuspendComplete{error}+Status{ACTIVE} on gate failure (+ releases slot on FinishSuspend failure); CP routes SuspendComplete.error -> FailedPrecondition leaving spawn ACTIVE; web unchanged (existing toast). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-13-fail-closed-suspend-design.md.

TestTmuxRelayLiveAttach now skips (not fails) unless docker daemon is reachable AND spawnery/agent:dev exists — documented prereqs. Was failing the full just-test gate at 'docker run' exit 125 when the image was orphaned. Verified: skips cleanly without prereqs; passes (4.6s) when met.

Delta-only export/import implemented on BOTH lanes (was: docker save / containerd Export shipped the full image, 176MB for a 50MB change). Docker lane: go-containerregistry — ExportTopLayer (top layer only, uncompressed for Kopia CDC dedup) + AssembleOnBase (daemon.Image base + mutate.AppendLayers + daemon.Write); LIVE-VERIFIED on a userns-remapped daemon via delta_e2e_test (22.9MB top-layer export, reassembled on base-only target, whiteout/file/pkg/uid all intact). CRI lane: content-store blob ExportTopLayer (decompress gzip->tar) + AssembleOnBase (write blob + shared assembleDeltaImage), unit-tested via fake engine. Deep research wf w1yxooapv confirmed docker save/load can't ship a layer against an existing base (moby#18723) and ruled out FROM+ADD (breaks whiteouts). CRI live-lane verification tracked as a P3 follow-up (gated on cloud-lane deploy). Commits 1eff143 (cri), prior (docker). Full gates green.

Live containerd delta-only round-trip VERIFIED + added to the e2e suite. cri_delta_e2e test (internal/runtime/cri/delta_cri_e2e_test.go) drives the real containerdEngine: Capture -> ExportTopLayer (3072B uncompressed delta vs 47MB base) -> drop delta image -> AssembleOnBase onto base -> UNPACK + RUN reassembled image -> whiteout applied + added file present. Confirms the uncompressed-tar delta layer (mediatype image.layer.v1.tar) materializes correctly via the overlayfs snapshotter (the rootfs runsc boots on). Runner: just test-cri-delta (dedicated containerd, root). Hermetic gates green; tagged test excluded from default build.

Container filesystem perms epic COMPLETE (16/16). Delivered: writable userspace via userns-remap (docker) / sentry-native (runsc) with default caps (never NET_ADMIN); storage chown-into-range replacing 0777; rootfs delta capture-on-suspend + resume-from-delta both lanes (docker commit / containerd diff), pinned base digests, squash/scrub/GC/quota/capture-before-reap; fail-closed suspend (pause-snapshot gate, abort+keep-ACTIVE on journal failure); delta-only migration export/import (go-containerregistry docker lane live-verified; containerd content-store CRI lane, live e2e green); cross-node migratable deltas via Kopia (sp-ei4.1.13). Research+spikes+design throughout (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-writable-rootfs-survival-*, 2026-06-13-*). All gates green; e2e: delta_e2e (docker), cri_delta_e2e (just test-cri-delta).

Fixed: storage.NormalizeOwnership(hostDir, agentUID) applied after journal.Restore in manager resume. Privileged node chowns restored tree into the agent uid preserving modes (0600 stays 0600, owned by agent-root); rootless node falls back to world-writable (matches Prepare's 0777 policy). Hermetic tests (privileged-preserves-mode / EPERM-world-writable / degraded-no-chown) + full suite green. Verify live: rebuild spawnlet, restart node, suspend+resume — restored 0600 files become agent-writable (rootless: shown as nobody but rw-rw-rw-; rootful: owned root, mode preserved).
